items.title: OKC looking to drive but looking to use those drives to poke at the Spurs rotations and Wemby. Wallace drives vs. Barnes, Wembanyama pulls into the paint to help. Skip pass is there to Caruso, rotation is there. Extra to Williams for 3.

items.title: Miller & Brazil are headliners for Kings workout tomorrow
items.author: Mark Jones
items.body_excerpt:
Miller & Brazil are headliners for Kings workout tomorrow

Miller started @ FSU. 

Scotty Barnes comp when he first got there…Big game at Kansas.. his signature game this yr.

Brazil is a freak athlete...initially played for Mussleman at Arkansas..Lob threat. Shot blocker. Live body. He’s switchable

Also, Wemby is First-Team All-NBA.

items.title: Jamal Murray makes his first All-NBA team. Scottie Barnes snubbed. Jalen Johnson didn’t have an overall better year than him imo. Luka and Cade becoming eligible hurt Barnes, Jaylen Brown (second team instead of first) and KAT, amongst others.
items.author: Wolstat
items.body_excerpt:
Jamal Murray makes his first All-NBA team. Scottie Barnes snubbed. Jalen Johnson didn’t have an overall better year than him imo. Luka and Cade becoming eligible hurt Barnes, Jaylen Brown (second team instead of first) and KAT, amongst others. 
No LeBron for first time in decades

items.author: Eric Koreen
items.body_excerpt: Scottie Barnes named second-team All-NBA, finishing sixth in overall voting, just shy of Derrick White for last first team spot.

items.title: All of the top-5 defenses had at least one All-Defense selection
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
All of the top-5 defenses had at least one All-Defense selection

1. OKC (Holmgren, Wallace)
2. DET (Thompson)
3. SA (Wembanyama)
4. BOS (White)
5. TOR (Barnes)

Other 4 selections/what team's defense ranked:

7. NY (Anunoby)
8. MIN (Gobert)
10. ATL (Daniels)
14. MIA (Adebayo)

items.title: Scottie Barnes named to second all-defensive NBA team, just third Raptors (Leonard, 2018; OG 2023) to ever make it. He finished sixth overall in voting with 42 first team votes, 46 second team votes and a mystifying 12 voters left him off ballots completely.
items.author: Doug Smith
items.body_excerpt: Scottie Barnes named to second all-defensive NBA team, just third Raptors (Leonard, 2018; OG 2023) to ever make it. He finished sixth overall in voting with 42 first team votes, 46 second team votes and a mystifying 12 voters left him off ballots completely.

items.title: All Defense 1st Team: Wemby - Chet - Ausar - Gobert - White 2nd Team: Barnes - Wallace - Adebayo - OG - Daniels
items.author: YourMomTheRedditor
items.body_excerpt: All Defense 1st Team: Wemby - Chet - Ausar - Gobert - White 2nd Team: Barnes - Wallace - Adebayo - OG - Daniels Congrats to all the winners this year!

Scottie Barnes has been named to All-Defense Second Team

Barnes finished sixth in total voting points, and just missed First Team

items.title: NBA All-Defensive Teams
items.author: Kurt Helin
items.body_excerpt:
NBA All-Defensive Teams

First Team
Victor Wembanyama (Spurs)
Chet Holmgren (Thunder)
Ausar Thompson (Pistons)
Rudy Gobert (Timberwolves)
Derrick White (Celtics)

Second Team
Bam Adebayo (Heat)
OG Anunoby (Knicks)
Scottie Barnes (Raptors)
Cason Wallace (Thunder)
Dyson Daniels (Hawks)

items.title: 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive teams:
items.author: Shams Charania Tweets
items.body_excerpt:
2025-26 NBA All-Defensive teams:

First team: Victor Wembanyama, Chet Holmgren, Ausar Thompson, Rudy Gobert, Derrick White

Second team: Bam Adebayo, Scottie Barnes, Cason Wallace, Dyson Daniels, 
OG Anunoby

items.title: Derrick White earns a spot on the All-Defensive First Team.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Derrick White earns a spot on the All-Defensive First Team.

-1/2 players with at least 80 steals & 80+ blocks (Scottie Barnes)

-3rd in defensive win shares

-Most shots contested among guards

-1st Celtic with 75+ blocks and 75+ steals in a season since when Kevin Garnett won the DPOY in 2007-08
